brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (1.94% ≈ 2%) via valid Bayes' theorem with correct arithmetic; goes one level beyond computation by explaining the base rate neglect intuition trap and why false positives dominate; explicitly names the suppressed failure mode (base rate neglect) and signals it's amplifying prior weighting, but does not course-correct mid-analysis or question its own method; shows full calculation chain but does not re-derive from a second angle (e.g., natural frequencies) or run a boundary condition test; correctly distinguishes the computation as deterministic but doesn't discuss what assumptions (e.g., test independence, representative sampling) could change the conclusion; mentions the wrong answer choices and explains why they fail, though without systematic evaluation of each alternative; every derivation step is explicit and reproducible — prior, both likelihoods, total P(positive), posterior numerator and denominator all shown; explicitly avoids the negative gate pattern by fully updating the prior with test evidence rather than anchoring on the base rate; zero evidence of either suppressed failure mode and explicitly names base rate neglect as the trap; follows the PRIOR→evidence→LR→Bayes→posterior chain from the topology but does not show the branching gates or the M1 primacy-bias check node.
